mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month jump in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off equity selling as recession probabilities spike within a week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly expansion in real GDP and month-over-month growth in housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macro environment, rising industrial production reflects firm pricing power and margin expansion, which directly boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 1.0% month-over-month, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in consumer credit balances reflects resilient household spending, providing immediate positive momentum for corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances when credit growth exceeds 0.5%, or rolling 1-year Sharpe ratios fall below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent headline inflation exceeding 3.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy for longer, depressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite inflation remaining above 3.5 percent for consecutive quarters, or the forward return distribution shows no statistical difference from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the unemployment rate and declines in industrial production indicate a demand-driven labor market breakdown, accelerating equity drawdowns as earnings expectations collapse within a month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward equity moves in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for volatility.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, rapid month-over-month growth in housing permits leads sector rotation into financials and industrials, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as cyclical valuations expand.
failure_condition: Housing permit surges coincide with equity drawdowns in more than 40% of observed episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for market volatility regimes.
